title: tagetes moorei var. moorei
intro: Tagetes moorei var. moorei, also known as Mexican Marigold, is a perennial plant native to Mexico, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Tagetes moorei var. moorei include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its antimicrobial properties which can aid in wound healing and prevent infections. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues.
